workflow: RupeeKit Workflow 1 - Keyword Research and Content Plan

plan.searchIntent: Help salaried employees calculate possible HRA exemption using salary, rent, city type, and HRA received.
plan.targetPersona: Salaried employees in India, tax planners, individuals seeking to optimize their tax liability.
plan.primaryGoal: Provide an accurate HRA exemption calculation tool and clear explanation for Indian taxpayers, driving organic traffic and user engagement.
titleOptions: HRA Exemption Calculator India - Calculate Your Tax Benefit
Calculate HRA Exemption in India | RupeeKit Tool
India HRA Exemption Calculator: Save Tax on Rent
plan.recommendedMetaTitle: HRA Exemption Calculator India - Calculate Your Tax Benefit
plan.recommendedMetaDescription: Calculate HRA exemption in India using salary, HRA received, rent paid, and metro or non-metro city rules.

inputs.name: basicSalary
inputs.label: Basic Salary (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter your monthly basic salary component.

inputs.name: dearnessAllowance
inputs.label: Dearness Allowance (DA) (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter your monthly Dearness Allowance. Include only if it forms part of retirement benefits.

inputs.name: commissionOnSales
inputs.label: Commission on Sales (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter monthly commission if it's a fixed percentage on turnover. Otherwise, enter 0.

inputs.name: hraReceived
inputs.label: Actual HRA Received (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter the actual House Rent Allowance you receive from your employer per month.

inputs.name: rentPaid
inputs.label: Actual Rent Paid (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter the actual rent you pay for your accommodation per month.

inputs.name: metroCityFactor
inputs.label: Metro City Factor (%)
inputs.helpText: Enter 50 for Metro cities (Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai). Enter 40 for other cities.

calculatorSpec.formulaPlainEnglish: The HRA exemption is the least of the following three amounts: 1. Actual HRA received. 2. 50% of salary (for metro cities) or 40% of salary (for non-metro cities). 3. Rent paid minus 10% of salary. 'Salary' for HRA calculation includes Basic Salary, Dearness Allowance (if part of retirement benefits), and Commission (if fixed percentage on turnover).
calculatorSpec.formula: min(hraReceived, (basicSalary + dearnessAllowance + commissionOnSales) * metroCityFactor / 100, max(0, rentPaid - (basicSalary + dearnessAllowance + commissionOnSales) * 10 / 100))

contentBrief.introAngle: Start by highlighting the tax-saving potential of HRA for salaried individuals in India and introduce the calculator as a quick, reliable tool to determine their eligible exemption.
mustCover: Definition of HRA and its purpose.
Eligibility criteria for claiming HRA exemption.
Detailed explanation of the three conditions for HRA exemption calculation.
What constitutes 'salary' for HRA calculation (Basic + DA + Commission).
Difference in calculation for Metro vs. Non-Metro cities.
Documents required for claiming HRA exemption (rent receipts, landlord PAN if rent > ₹1 Lakh/year).
Impact of HRA exemption on overall taxable income.
Scenarios where HRA cannot be claimed (e.g., living in own house, not paying rent).
Brief mention of Old vs. New Tax Regime implications for HRA.
contentBrief.exampleScenario: Priya lives in Bengaluru (non-metro for HRA calculation purposes, factor 40%). Her basic salary is ₹60,000, DA is ₹15,000, and she receives ₹25,000 as HRA. She pays ₹30,000 in rent. Calculate her HRA exemption.
faqQuestions: Who is eligible to claim HRA exemption?
What is considered 'salary' for HRA calculation?
Can I claim HRA if I live with my parents and pay rent to them?
What documents do I need to claim HRA exemption?
Is HRA exemption available under the New Tax Regime?
What if my HRA received is less than the rent I pay?
Do I need my landlord's PAN card for HRA exemption?
trustNotes: Uses commonly followed Indian HRA exemption calculation rules for educational purposes.
Explains assumptions clearly so users can understand how the estimate is calculated.
Designed as an informational calculator, not as personalized tax or financial advice.
Users should verify final tax treatment with official guidance or a qualified tax professional.
contentBrief.disclaimer: This calculator is for educational and informational purposes only. It provides an estimate based on the inputs and assumptions shown. It is not tax, legal, investment, or financial advice. Please verify with official sources or consult a qualified professional before making tax or financial decisions.
